en.extract:
The lesser moorhen (Paragallinula angulata) is a species of bird in the family Rallidae. It is sometimes placed into the genus Gallinula. It is the only member of the genus Paragallinula.


Taxonomy
The lesser moorhen was formerly placed in the genus Gallinula, but a 2015 molecular genetic study demonstrated that Gallinula was composed of four distinct lineages. The genus was split into four genera: Gallinula (sensu stricto), Paragallinula, Porphyriops, and Tribonyx.
P. angulata is the only known species of Paragallinula which makes Paragallinula a monotypic genus.
The name paragallinula comes from the Greek para 'beside' and the genus Gallinula. It is named for its similarity with the members of Gallinula, but it also indicates its unique genetic lineage.


Distribution
The lesser moorhen is widely spread across Sub-Saharan Africa (excluding Southern Africa and Madagascar). It was once recorded in the Western Palearctic as a rare vagrant. This species has also been sighted in Brazil, Egypt, Morocco, and Oman.


References


External links
Lesser moorhen - Species text in The Atlas of Southern African Birds

The '''lesser moorhen''' ('''''Paragallinula angulata''''') is a species of bird in the family [[Rallidae]]. It is sometimes placed into the genus ''[[Gallinula]]''. It is the only member of the genus '''''Paragallinula'''''.<ref name=ioc>{{cite web| editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | year=2019 | title=Flufftails, finfoots, rails, trumpeters, cranes, limpkin | work=World Bird List Version 9.2 | url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/flufftails/ | publisher=International Ornithologists' Union | access-date=26 June 2019 }}</ref>

== Taxonomy ==
The lesser moorhen was formerly placed in the genus ''[[Gallinula]],'' but a 2015 molecular genetic study demonstrated that ''Gallinula'' was composed of four distinct lineages. The genus was split into four genera: ''Gallinula ([[sensu stricto]]), Paragallinula, [[Porphyriops]],'' and ''[[Tribonyx]].''<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|last1=Sangster|first1=George|last2=Garcia-R|first2=Juan Carlos|last3=Trewick|first3=Steve A.|date=2015-11-16|title=A new genus for the Lesser Moorhen Gallinula angulata Sundevall, 1850 (Aves, Rallidae)|url=https://europeanjournaloftaxonomy.eu/index.php/ejt/article/view/275|journal=European Journal of Taxonomy|language=en|issue=153|doi=10.5852/ejt.2015.153|issn=2118-9773|doi-access=free}}</ref>

''P. angulata'' is the only known species of ''Paragallinula'' which makes ''Paragallinula'' a [[Monotypic taxon|monotypic]] genus.

The name paragallinula comes from the Greek ''para'' 'beside' and the genus ''Gallinula.'' It is named for its similarity with the members of ''Gallinula'', but it also indicates its unique genetic lineage.<ref name=":0" />

== Distribution ==
The lesser moorhen is widely spread across [[Sub-Saharan Africa]] (excluding Southern Africa and Madagascar).<ref name="iucn status 11 November 2021"/> It was once recorded in the Western Palearctic as a rare vagrant.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2019-03-09 |title=Lesser Moorhen in Cape Verde, March 2019 |url=https://www.birdguides.com/articles/lesser-moorhen-in-cape-verde-march-2019/ |access-date=2025-04-03 |website=BirdGuides }}</ref> This species has also been sighted in [[Brazil]], [[Egypt]], [[Morocco]], and [[Oman]].<ref name="iucn status 11 November 2021"/><ref>{{Cite web |last=MaghrebOrnitho |date=2025-03-19 |title=First record of Lesser Moorhen for Morocco |url=https://magornitho.org/2025/03/first-lesser-moorhen-morocco/ |access-date=2025-04-03 |website=MaghrebOrnitho |language=en-GB}}</ref>
